description: Public opinion on the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act during its final legislative push in the US. Despite a strong initial push, opposition grew significantly as tea party protests gained momentum leading to the March 2010 signing.

key_events.description: August 2009 Town Hall protests increase media focus on government overreach.
January 2010 Scott Brown wins Massachusetts Senate seat, breaking supermajority.
March 2010 ACA signed into law by President Obama.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling legislative polarization and public sentiment volatility during major infrastructure changes.
